UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION FOR (1) IIED- PLAINTIFF FAILED TO ALLEGE FACTS RE: OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT AND SEVERE EMOTIONAL DISTRESS; (2) DECLARATORY AND INJUNCTIVE RELIEF- PLAINTIFF FAILED TO ALLEGE TENDER OR APPLICABLE EXCEPTION FROM TENDER.SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO (1) NIED - TO ALLEGE DUTY; AND (2) UCL VIOLATION TO ALLEGE INJURY IN FACT.OVERRULED AS TO (1) BREACH OF CONTRACT - PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED BREACH OF "NO-FEE" REFINANCE PROVISION IN THREE MONTHS OF SIGNING, I.E. MARCH, 2008. 4 YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS APPLIES; (2) BREACH OF COVENANT- DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER DOES NOT ADDRESS ALLEGATIONS RE: WRITTEN CONTRACT; (3) FRAUD - SEE ALLEGATIONS SAC PAGES, 36-37; (4) PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL - DEFENDANT DOES NOT CITE ANY AUTHORITY RE: APPLICABILITY OF STATUTE OF FRAUDS TO PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL, WHERE A PROMISE TO OFFER A MODIFICATION IS ALLEGED. DEFENDANT AND ITS COUNSEL ARE ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH ALL PROVISIONS OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT AND LOCAL RULES OF COURT REGARDING FILING, NOTICING AND SERVING OF THE MOTIONS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 AND APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE HARRY M. DORFMAN, CLERK N. BAUTISTA, NOT REPORTED, (501)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-20-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Atty. Victoria Edwards (303-260-7712) appearing for Defendant and moving party apperaing in Court Call/ Plaintiff in Propera Persona Mohsin Ahmmed (415-314-1715) and opposing party.

Argued. - Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part as follows: Sustained without leave to amend as to causes of action for (1) IIED- Plaintiff failed to allege facts re: outrageous conduct and severe emotional distress; (2) Declaratory and Injunctive relief- plaintiff failed to allege tender or applicable exception from tender.Sustained with leave to amend as to (1) NIED - to allege duty; and (2) UCL violation to allege injury in fact.Overruled as to (1) breach of contract - Plaintiff sufficiently alleged breach of "no-fee" refinance provision in three months of signing, i.e. March, 2008. 4 year Statute of limitations applies; (2) breach of covenant- defendant's demurrer does not address allegations re: written contract; (3) fraud - See allegations Sac pages, 36-37; (4) promissory estoppel - defendant does not cite any authority re: applicability of Statute of Frauds to promissory estoppel, where a promise to offer a modification is alleged.

In Ruling on this Motion the Court considered the following filings (1) "Notice of Continued Hearing" filed on August 2, 2013; (2) Demurrer filed on June 27, 2013; (3) Memorandum of Points and Authorities in support of Demurrer field on June 27, 2013; (4) Request for Judicial Notice filed on June 27, 2013; (5) Notice of Errata Regarding Defendant's Demurrer filed on July 2, 2013; (5) Opposition to Demurrer filed on August 21, 2013; (6) Plaintiff's request for Judicial Notice filed on August 21, 2013; (7) Plaintiff's Objections to Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice. No Reply was filed in support of Demurrer filed on August 2, 2013. Defendant's continued disregard of the Court's Local Rules and directions given by the Court has created confusion as to bringing and calendaring of the motions. Defendant's Demurrer to the Second Amended Complaint and Motion to Strike were ordered off calendar on August 2, 2013 by the Honorable Marla J. Miller. Later on August 2, 2013, Defendant filed a "Notice of Continued Hearing" on Defendant's Demurrer. No attempt to re-file Motion to Strike was made. Defendant filed an Opposition to the purported re-calendared Demurrer on August 21, 2013. Defendant and its counsel are ordered to comply with all provisions of the Code of Civil Procedure, California Rules of Court and Local Rules of Court regarding filing, noticing and serving of the motions. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and approved as to form. Judge Harry M. Dorfman, Clerk N. Bautista, Not Reported, (501)
